steps.title: Translate the problem to standard form
steps.content:
The product of $x$ and $(x-7)$ equals 228. Writing this as an equation:
$$x(x - 7) = 228$$
Expand the left side and rearrange to standard form:
$$x^2 - 7x = 228$$
$$x^2 - 7x - 228 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor by finding compatible numbers
steps.content:
For $x^2 - 7x - 228 = 0$, we need two numbers that multiply to $-228$ and add to $-7$. Since $228 = 12 \times 19$, test $(-19)$ and \$12$:
$$(-19) \times 12 = -228 \quad \text{and} \quad (-19) + 12 = -7$$
Therefore, the factorization is:
$$(x - 19)(x + 12) = 0$$
Solving: $x = 19$ or $x = -12$.

steps.title: Select the positive solution
steps.content:
Since the problem specifies that $x$ is positive, $x = 19$.
Verify: $19 \times (19 - 7) = 19 \times 12 = 228$. ✓
